summary: Bryan Cantrill announces his recognition as one of MIT Technology Review's TR35, the top 35 innovators under 35. He expresses genuine humility about the honor, noting that other recipients are working on seemingly more significant problems like cancer cures and rocket science. The post is characteristically self-deprecating while acknowledging the achievement.
key_points: Named to MIT Technology Review's TR35 list of top innovators under 35
Expresses humility by comparing his work to cancer research and rocket science
References a podcast interview about the award
money_quotes: It's a great honor, especially because the other honorees are actually working on things like cures for cancer and rocket science – domains that I have known only as rhetorical flourish.
Should you like to hear me make a jackass out of myself on the subject, you might want to check out Richard Giles's latest I/O podcast.
key_insight: Even significant industry recognition feels humbling when viewed alongside work in fields like medicine and aerospace that have more immediate life-or-death implications.
